mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of industrial production contraction exceeding 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate persistent inflation that forces continued monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% alongside non-declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of aggregate income expansion and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth rates across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly gross domestic product contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that override short-term technical factors, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and payroll expansion signals structural labor market inefficiencies that erode profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during economic slowdowns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth falls below PAYEMS growth by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the INDPRO-PAYEMS growth differential falls below -0.5 percentage points.
